g.data-narration: Welcome to learning about interrupt assignment in 8051 microcontrollers. This topic is essential for embedded systems design.
An interrupt is an asynchronous signal that temporarily suspends the main program to handle a specific event or condition.
g.text: An asynchronous event that temporarily suspends main program execution
to handle specific conditions like external signals or timer overflows.

g.data-narration: Compare polling (continuously checking devices) with interrupt-driven approach (responding to events when they occur).
The 8051 has five interrupt sources: two external, two timer interrupts, and one serial port interrupt.
Each interrupt has a fixed vector address in program memory where the CPU jumps when the interrupt occurs.
The IE register controls interrupt enabling. The EA bit must be 1 along with specific interrupt enable bits to activate interrupts.
g.text: ES = Serial, ET1/ET0 = Timers, EX1/EX0 = External

g.data-narration: The IP register assigns priority levels. Setting a bit to 1 gives high priority, 0 gives low priority to that interrupt.
The TCON register configures external interrupts as level-triggered or edge-triggered using IT0 and IT1 bits.
An ISR is a special function executed in response to an interrupt. In C, use the interrupt keyword and volatile for shared variables.
g.text: Use volatile for shared vars • End with RETI • Keep it short

g.data-narration: When an interrupt occurs: finish current instruction, push PC to stack, jump to vector address, execute ISR, then RETI.
This example enables external interrupt 0 as edge-triggered and configures the IE register to enable global and EX0 interrupts.
For timer interrupts, calculate initial value: Initial equals 65536 minus delay divided by machine cycle.
Remember key formulas: IE needs EA plus specific bit, timer value calculation, machine cycle formula, and vector addresses.
Avoid common mistakes: forgetting EA bit, not reloading timer, using wrong return instruction, and level-triggered interrupt flag clearing.
g.text: X Forgetting EA bit in IE
X Not reloading timer in ISR
X Using RETURN instead of RETI
